overview: Refactor the internal processdefinition service to reduce duplication across supported Camunda versions, preserve existing search/latest/get behavior, explicitly review generated-client coverage, and complete the work with focused service tests plus repository validation.

userStories.title: Stabilize the shared processdefinition service contract
userStories.description: Clarify the shared API, versioned client contracts, and factory expectations for the processdefinition service so the refactor has a stable, repository-native surface to preserve.
acceptanceCriteria: The shared processdefinition API in `internal/services/processdefinition/api.go` reflects the preserved service surface without changing package layout or version selection behavior.

Version-specific contracts in `internal/services/processdefinition/v87/contract.go` and `internal/services/processdefinition/v88/contract.go` cover the methods needed for the approved service surface.
Factory expectations in `internal/services/processdefinition/factory.go` and `internal/services/processdefinition/factory_test.go` remain aligned with the preserved version-selection behavior.
userStories.notes: Keep this slice limited to shared contract and wiring clarity. Reuse the existing issue branch `67-processdefinition-api-refactor` and existing service/factory patterns.

userStories.title: Refactor the versioned processdefinition services without behavior change
userStories.description: Simplify duplicated request construction, response validation, and latest-result handling across the v8.7 and v8.8 processdefinition services while preserving all current observable behavior and supported version differences.
acceptanceCriteria: Search, latest, and get behavior in `internal/services/processdefinition/v87/service.go` remains unchanged, including rejection of unsupported statistics requests.
Search, latest, and get behavior in `internal/services/processdefinition/v88/service.go` remains unchanged, including current statistics enrichment behavior.
The refactor reduces avoidable duplication without introducing new packages, new dependency layers, or behavioral regressions in existing processdefinition paths.
userStories.notes: Use small shared helper extraction or localized cleanup only where it makes the code clearer. Preserve current mixed-version semantics rather than forcing convergence.

userStories.title: Complete the generated-client coverage decision
userStories.description: Review the supported generated processdefinition clients against the shared service surface and either add one bounded missing capability, with XML retrieval as the leading candidate, or record an explicit no-addition decision.
acceptanceCriteria: The generated-client coverage decision is explicit and traceable in the shared service surface and version-specific implementations.
Any added capability is supported in both v8.7 and v8.8, fits the current service boundaries, and does not require package or layout changes.
If no capability is added, the implementation and notes make the bounded no-addition decision explicit instead of leaving the review implicit.
userStories.notes: Treat XML retrieval as the leading candidate because both supported generated clients expose it, but do not force that outcome if the bounded review shows it should remain deferred.

userStories.title: Lock down regression coverage and final validation
userStories.description: Update focused processdefinition tests to protect the refactored behavior, version-specific differences, and final capability decision, then complete targeted validation and repository-wide verification.
acceptanceCriteria: Focused tests in `internal/services/processdefinition/factory_test.go`, `internal/services/processdefinition/v87/service_test.go`, and `internal/services/processdefinition/v88/service_test.go` cover the preserved behavior and final capability decision.
If the final capability decision introduces a user-visible workflow, the affected source documentation is updated and generated CLI docs are regenerated from repository tooling.
userStories.notes: Prefer tests that verify preserved external behavior rather than internal structure. Only touch documentation if the final coverage decision changes user-visible behavior.
